what_it_does: Static generated gallery page that indexes design mockups for a product called 'the company', grouping them into seven areas (A–G), showing each view's title, ID, target devices, implementation status (grounded/planned/build-required), and feature-rep tags, with grounded cards linking to resizable HTML mockups.
observations: It is a generated file produced by _system/gallery.py and advertises that status is derived from file existence.
Contains inline CSS plus a link to design-system.css; uses CSS custom properties such as --acc, --line, and --s1.
Organizes 43 views across 7 areas: A operating surface, B right-hand-man, C inbox/decisions, D self-build/wire, E models/embeddings, F frame/settings, G responsive.
Cards use status badges: grounded ✓, planned, and build-required.
Grounded cards are <a> elements linking to mockups/*.html; planned and build-required cards are non-link <div> placeholders.
Each card lists feature rep codes (e.g., EVT-now, NODE-kinds, WIRE-review) suggesting traceability to a feature inventory.
Device tags include desktop, mobile, tablet, and responsive.
The page displays generated summary counts: 15 grounded, 43 views, 7 areas.
Uses Bricolage Grotesque and IBM Plex Mono typography.
Thumbnail placeholders are textual rather than image previews.
Several <div class=reps> attributes are written without quotes.
standouts: Status is machine-derived from whether the corresponding mockup file exists, and the page claims it 'cannot drift'.
Grounded cards are live links; ungrounded cards are inert placeholders, making the gallery itself a build-status dashboard.
Feature-rep tags on every card map design views to an abstract feature vocabulary (EVT, NODE, RUN, ENG, RHM, etc.).
The corpus is framed as 'the operating surface' of a self-building, model-orchestrated system.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties for theming and a separate design-system.css.
Compact, utility-style class names such as .card, .thumb, .meta, .b, and .st.
Generated static HTML with no runtime JavaScript.
Lowercase, space-separated headings with an <em> styled for the brand word.
HTML5 doctype and a viewport meta tag.
concerns: Several attributes are written as class=reps without quotes, which is fragile and inconsistent with the rest of the markup.
Generated counts (15 grounded, 43 views) are hardcoded and could become stale if the generator is not run.
Thumbnail placeholders are text-only, so the gallery gives no visual preview of the mockups.
Status colors rely partly on color alone, with only small text labels for accessibility.
No visible timestamp or provenance for when the page was last generated.
notes: A generated design-corpus dashboard that doubles as a build-status report, linking grounded mockups and exposing which views still need implementation.
questions: Where exactly is _system/gallery.py relative to this file, and how does it detect file existence?
What do the feature-rep abbreviations (EVT, NODE, RUN, ENG, RHM, WIRE, etc.) map to in the feature inventory?
Are there separate mobile mockup files, or do the mobile tags merely indicate responsive intent?
How is a view classified as 'planned' versus 'build-required'?
Why does area G (Responsive system) have zero grounded views despite many earlier cards claiming mobile support?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: it presents itself as a generated, file-backed gallery of design views and does so, listing areas, statuses, and links to existing mockups.
apparent_themes: Design-as-inventory: every view is catalogued and traced to feature codes.
Grounded design: a view is not real until its mockup file exists.
AI co-pilot / right-hand-man interface (RHM, twin, walkthrough, presence).
Model fleet and embedding/retrieval as first-class UI surfaces.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the design system auditable and self-synchronizing: a single page where stakeholders can see which product surfaces are already mocked up, which are planned, and which design concepts trace to which implementation features, with the gallery itself regenerated from the file system so it cannot diverge.
novelty: The use of a generated gallery where each card's grounded/planned/build-required status is derived from the presence of a mockup file, combined with compact feature-rep tags that create a design-to-feature traceability layer.
contribution: It contributes a navigable, status-aware index that connects design mockups to a feature vocabulary and communicates implementation gaps without requiring a separate project-management tool.
summary_for_embedding: A generated HTML design corpus gallery for 'the company' that groups mockup views into seven functional areas, links grounded mockups, and tags each view with device targets and feature-rep codes.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a self-updating, single-source-of-truth dashboard of the product's design surface and its implementation status.
